svg.text: Enterprise Multi-Region DR Architecture — Two Independent DR Layers
DR Layer 1: Application DR (Route53 user traffic) | DR Layer 2: Network DR (On-Prem connectivity via dual TGW + BGP VPN)
DR LAYER 1 — Application DR (User Traffic Only) — Route53 Failover DNS
PRIMARY → Tokyo ALB | SECONDARY → Osaka ALB | TTL 30s | HC every 10s
Layer 1 is SELF-CONTAINED. Each ALB serves users independently. No cross-region dependency. Failover = DNS record flip only.
DR LAYER 2 — Network DR (On-Prem Connectivity) — Dual TGW + BGP VPN — COMPLETELY SEPARATE FROM LAYER 1
VPN ECMP ON · BGP dynamic routing · Manual RT management
TgwStack creates: TGW · VPC Attachment · Customer Gateways · VPN Connections · vpc-rt · vpn-rt
BGP sessions are always UP. Tokyo path preferred via LOCAL_PREF 200 on on-prem router.
Failover: Tokyo TGW BGP drops → on-prem reconverges to Osaka TGW. Tokyo VPC becomes unreachable from on-prem.
Recovery: Tokyo TGW BGP restores → on-prem prefers Tokyo again (LOCAL_PREF 200 wins).
VPN ECMP ON · BGP dynamic routing · Manual RT management
Osaka TGW is fully independent. Always connected to on-prem via its own VPN sessions.
Osaka VPN LOCAL_PREF 100 → standby path. On-prem prefers Tokyo (200) at steady state.
When Tokyo TGW fails: on-prem BGP reconverges here automatically. No Lambda. No route swap.
Osaka VPC serves on-prem traffic independently during Tokyo outage.
Traffic Flow — Normal State vs. Failover State (DR Layer 2: On-Prem Connectivity)
On-prem → AWS: On-prem router → Tokyo TGW VPN (LOCAL_PREF 200) → Tokyo TGW vpc-rt → Tokyo VPC
AWS → On-prem: Tokyo VPC → Tokyo TGW → vpn-rt → Tokyo VPN → On-prem router
Osaka TGW VPN sessions are ALWAYS UP but idle (LOCAL_PREF 100 = not preferred by BGP).
TGW Inter-Region Peering active for Tokyo VPC ↔ Osaka VPC internal traffic (separate concern).
Layer 1: Users → Route53 → Tokyo ALB (PRIMARY record active).
Layer 1 and Layer 2 are independent. Either can fail without affecting the other.
No Lambda. No route swap. BGP handles Layer 2. Route53 handles Layer 1.
① Tokyo TGW BGP session drops (hold-time expires ~30s).
② On-prem router withdraws Tokyo routes from its BGP table.
③ BGP reconverges: on-prem → Osaka TGW VPN (LOCAL_PREF 100, now best path).
④ On-prem → Osaka TGW vpc-rt → Osaka VPC. Osaka serves on-prem traffic.
Tokyo VPC is unreachable from on-prem (Tokyo TGW is down). This is expected.
Layer 1: Route53 detects Tokyo ALB failure → DNS flips to Osaka ALB (~20s).
Recovery: Tokyo TGW restores → BGP prefers Tokyo again (LOCAL_PREF 200 wins automatically).
KEY: The on-prem router switches between TGWs via BGP. Tokyo VPC never needs to reach Osaka TGW directly. No cross-region VPC attachment required.
